symptoms.symptom_name: Rigors with rapid deterioration on oral antibiotics for UTI

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Core criterion for sepsis-related organ dysfunction.
Core criterion for sepsis-related organ dysfunction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced urine output despite oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sepsis with early acute kidney injury

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension, delayed capillary refill, mottled skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rigors, new confusion, tachypnoea, and reduced urine output together confirm sepsis with organ dysfunction requiring emergency IV treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate IV fluid resuscitation and broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (the 'Sepsis Six' bundle)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Time-critical management; each hour of delay in antibiotics measurably increases sepsis mortality.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood cultures, serum lactate, and urine culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the causative organism and quantifies severity, but should not delay treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Switching to a different oral antibiotic and reviewing again tomorrow
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay recognition and treatment of established sepsis.
